results.title: Tines Support Reference Guide | Tines
results.content:
## 6. Bugs

All queries and issues that cannot be resolved will be referred to the Tines product development team. The design and development teams review all product bugs and prioritize resolution in a future update. Customers will be informed of the plan to resolve a bug.

## 7. Ideas/Feature Requests

New Tines features and updates are posted at 

Ideas for improving the Tines platform's functionality are warmly welcomed and can be submitted to the Tines support team. We genuinely appreciate every idea submitted — your feedback plays a valuable role in shaping the platform's future. [...] ## 8. Ensuring Customer Satisfaction

At Tines, we welcome your feedback, value your comments, and encourage you to contact us with any concerns or suggestions. In addition, as part of our commitment to providing you with the best experience possible, Tines conducts customer surveys. From time to time, after a case has been closed or as part of an NPS survey, you may be requested to provide information regarding your satisfaction with the quality of our products and services. Please take the few minutes necessary to answer the questions to help us maintain the highest level of service.

### Service Performance

As part of our commitment to transparency, Tines publishes a quarterly summary of support performance. The table below reflects the last 3 months of service delivery: [...] When a query is raised, support may:

Request a copy or sample of the story, action, or event to which the query relates

Request access to the tenant the query relates to

Request a remote meeting with the user who raised the query to assist with investigations

Consult with other Tines colleagues to determine the nature of the issue or query

Require further information to assist with investigations

Provide a workaround while a permanent resolution is prepared

Recommend an upgrade to the latest version of Tines to address the query or issue (Self-Hosted only)

### Tines Status Page

If an issue with Tines affects multiple cloud-hosted customers, details will be provided at  regarding the status, resolution, and cause.

We recommend that all users subscribe to the status page.

results.title: Tine Alignment: Tips for Smooth Writing – Fountain Pen Revolution
results.content:
| Misalignment Symptom | Cause | Ink Flow Impact | Correction Method |
 ---  --- |
| Scratchy in one direction | One tine is higher than the other | Disrupted flow; edges catch fibers | Gently nudge the higher tine down using a fingernail or card |
| Skips or hard starts | Tines too tight or "Baby's Bottom" | Restricted flow; ink fails to reach paper | Widen the tine gap or regrind the tipping (for Baby's Bottom) |
| Excessive wetness/blobbing | Tines too far apart (wide gap) | Overflows; ink pools on the page | Carefully cross the tines to reduce the gap |
| Scratchy in all directions | Damaged tipping or misalignment | Poor flow; ink doesn’t lubricate properly | Align the tines, then smooth lightly with micromesh | [...] Free US Shipping on orders $65!

Free International Shipping Over $149\

Fountain Pen Revolution

# Tine Alignment: Tips for Smooth Writing

Tine Alignment: Tips for Smooth Writing
Tine Alignment: Tips for Smooth Writing

Kevin Thiemann |
December 29, 2025

When your fountain pen feels scratchy or skips, the likely culprit is misaligned tines - the two halves of the nib that guide ink to the paper. Proper alignment ensures smooth writing by allowing the nib's tipping material to glide evenly. Here's how to fix it:

For beginners, practice on inexpensive pens before attempting adjustments on valuable ones. If the problem persists or the nib is damaged, consult a professional. Taking the time to align your nib ensures a smoother and more enjoyable writing experience. [...] If your fountain pen feels scratchy, skips, or only writes smoothly in one direction - like left to right - the tines might be out of alignment. To check, examine the nib carefully under bright light or with a magnifying glass. If one tine sits higher or lower than the other, you've likely found the problem. When the tines are properly aligned, the ink flows smoothly, making writing much more enjoyable.

### What do I need to align fountain pen tines at home?

Aligning the tines of a fountain pen at home is easier than you might think, and you only need a few simple tools to get started:

With these basic items, you can realign the tines and get your fountain pen writing smoothly again - all from the comfort of home.

### Nutrition for the body. A smile for the tastebuds.

##

### BROKEN TINE ORCHARD IS THE HOME OF THE CANADIAN SUPERFRUIT, HASKAP BERRY

The farm is situated southwest of Beaverlodge, Alberta, Canada, along the Rocky Mountain foothills. Currently cultivated land is approximately 2400 acres – with the main crops being canola, wheat, and barley. In 2011, we planted a Haskap orchard to start diversification and help in the succession of this proud farm. We are a 4th generation farm – my family homesteading the land in 1926, with the Haskap orchard located on the original cleared land of the homestead.
